ci_support_linux_64_python3.10.____cpyth:
c_compiler:
- gcc
c_compiler_version:
- '14'
c_stdlib:
- sysroot
c_stdlib_version:
- '2.17'
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cxx_compiler:
- gxx
cxx_compiler_version:
- '14'
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
libxml2_devel:
- '2.14'
pin_run_as_build:
  python:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
python:
- 3.10.* *_cpython
target_platform:
- linux-64
zip_keys:
- - c_compiler_version
  - cxx_compiler_version
name: pyside6
raw_meta_yaml:
context:
  version: "6.11.0"

  # While this is typically "major and minor" on PySide 6.8.0
  # There were two "emergency" releases 6.8.0.1 and 6.8.0.2 that came out quickly
  # and revealed that this number can contain a 3rd number under the right circumstances
  maj_min: ${{ (version | split('.'))[:-1] | join('.') }}
  maj_min_patch: ${{ (version | split('.'))[:3] | join('.') }}

package:
  name: pyside6
  version: ${{ version }}

source:
  git: https://github.com/qtproject/pyside-pyside-setup.git
  tag: v${{ version }}

  patches:
    # https://bugreports.qt.io/browse/PYSIDE-1193
    - patches/pyi_osx.patch
    - patches/qfp_osx.patch

    # https://bugreports.qt.io/browse/PYSIDE-2057
    - patches/do_not_store_build_time_compiler.patch

    # change tools module location
    - patches/tools.patch

    # https://github.com/pyqtgraph/pyqtgraph/issues/3265
    - patches/0001-Fix-memory-leak.patch

    - patches/no_abi3.patch

build:
  number: 0
  python:
    entry_points:
      - pyside6-rcc = PySide6.scripts.pyside_tool:rcc
      - pyside6-uic = PySide6.scripts.pyside_tool:uic
    skip_pyc_compilation:
      # SyntaxError in jinja template
      - "*/__init__.tmpl.py"

requirements:
  build:
    - cmake
    - ninja
    - ${{ stdlib('c') }}
    - ${{ compiler('c') }}
    - ${{ compiler('cxx') }}
    - if: build_platform != target_platform
      then:
        - python
        - setuptools
        - libxslt
        - libxml2-devel
        - qt6-main ${{ maj_min_patch }}.*
    - if: not osx and (build_platform != target_platform)
      then:
        # Pin clang<22.x, see PYSIDE-3286
        - libclang 21.*
        - llvmdev 21.*
        - clangdev 21.*
    - if: osx and (build_platform != target_platform)
      then:
        - llvmdev ${{ cxx_compiler_version }}.*
        - clangdev ${{ cxx_compiler_version }}.*
        - libcxx ${{ cxx_compiler_version }}.*
        - libclang ${{ cxx_compiler_version }}.*

  host:
    - python
    - setuptools
    - libxslt
    - libxml2-devel
    - qt6-main ${{ maj_min_patch }}.*
    - qt6-3d
    - qt6-charts
    - qt6-datavis3d
    - qt6-graphs
    # Provides QtMultimediaWidgets and QtSpatialAudio
    - qt6-multimedia
    - qt6-networkauth
    - qt6-quick3d
    - if: not osx
      then:
        # Pin clang<22.x, see PYSIDE-3286
        - llvmdev 21.*
        - clangdev 21.*
        - libclang 21.*
    - if: linux
      then:
        - qt6-wayland
        - libgl-devel
        - libegl-devel
        - libopengl-devel
    - if: osx
      then:
        - llvmdev  ${{ cxx_compiler_version }}.*
        - clangdev ${{ cxx_compiler_version }}.*
        - libcxx  ${{ cxx_compiler_version }}.*
        - libclang  ${{ cxx_compiler_version }}.*
      else:
        - libvulkan-headers
        - libvulkan-loader
  run:
    - python
    - qt6-main ${{ maj_min_patch }}.*
  ignore_run_exports:
    from_package:
      # make these optional for slimmer downloads and licensing choice
      - qt6-3d
      - qt6-charts
      - qt6-datavis3d
      - qt6-graphs
      - qt6-wayland
      - qt6-multimedia
      - qt6-networkauth
      - qt6-quick3d

tests:
  - python:
      imports:
        #- PySide6.QtBluetooth
        - PySide6.QtConcurrent
        - PySide6.QtCore
        - PySide6.QtDesigner
        - PySide6.QtGui
        - PySide6.QtHelp
        #- PySide6.QtHttpServer
        - PySide6.QtNetwork
        #- PySide6.QtNfc
        - PySide6.QtOpenGL
        - PySide6.QtOpenGLWidgets
        #- PySide6.QtPdf
        #- PySide6.QtPdfWidgets
        # - PySide6.QtPositioning
        - PySide6.QtPrintSupport
        - PySide6.QtQml
        - PySide6.QtQuick
        - PySide6.QtQuickControls2
        - PySide6.QtQuickWidgets
        #- PySide6.QtRemoteObjects
        # - PySide6.QtScxml
        # - PySide6.QtSensors
        # - PySide6.QtSerialPort
        - PySide6.QtSql
        #- PySide6.QtStateMachine
        - PySide6.QtSvg
        - PySide6.QtSvgWidgets
        - PySide6.QtTest
        #- PySide6.QtTextToSpeech
        - PySide6.QtUiTools
        - PySide6.QtWebChannel
        #- PySide6.QtWebEngineCore
        #- PySide6.QtWebEngineQuick
        #- PySide6.QtWebEngineWidgets
        - PySide6.QtWebSockets
        - PySide6.QtWidgets
        - PySide6.QtXml
  - script:
    - shiboken6 --help
    - pyside6-rcc -help
    - pyside6-uic -help
    # Star imports have broken many times on PySide6
    # https://github.com/spyder-ide/qtpy/issues/494
    # https://github.com/spyder-ide/qtpy/issues/480
    # We add it here for safety so we don't release really broken packages
    - python -c "import PySide6; from PySide6.QtCore import *"
    - if: unix
      then:
        - test -f ${PREFIX}/lib/qt6/plugins/designer/libPySidePlugin${SHLIB_EXT}
        # Regression to avoid clobber with qt-main package
        - test ! -f $PREFIX/bin/uic
      else:
        - if exist %LIBRARY_BIN%\uic.exe exit 1
    - if: match(python, "<=3.10")
      then:
        - python -c "import PySide6.Qt3DAnimation"
        - python -c "import PySide6.Qt3DCore"
        - python -c "import PySide6.Qt3DExtras"
        - python -c "import PySide6.Qt3DInput"
        - python -c "import PySide6.Qt3DLogic"
        - python -c "import PySide6.Qt3DRender"
        - python -c "import PySide6.QtCharts"
        - python -c "import PySide6.QtDataVisualization"
        - python -c "import PySide6.QtGraphs"
        - python -c "import PySide6.QtMultimedia"
        - python -c "import PySide6.QtMultimediaWidgets"
        - python -c "import PySide6.QtNetworkAuth"
        - python -c "import PySide6.QtMultimediaWidgets"
        - python -c "import PySide6.QtQuick3D"
        - python -c "import PySide6.QtSpatialAudio"
    requirements:
      run:
        - if: match(python, "<=3.10")
          then:
            # these are optional
            # test them with one version of python
            - qt6-3d
            - qt6-charts
            - qt6-datavis3d
            - qt6-graphs
            - qt6-multimedia
            - qt6-networkauth

about:
  homepage: https://wiki.qt.io/PySide2
  license: LGPL-3.0-only
  license_file: LICENSES/LGPL-3.0-only.txt
  summary: Python bindings for Qt

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - jan-janssen
    - jschueller
